message:
feat(server): add turnkey HTTP server with Streamable HTTP transport

Introduce run_http / run_http_with_cx / run_http_returning variants on
Server that bind a TCP listener and serve MCP JSON-RPC over plain HTTP,
mirroring the ergonomics of the existing run_stdio family.

Key additions in crates/fastmcp-server/src/lib.rs:

- HttpServerConfig: builder-style configuration struct exposing
  mcp_path ("/mcp"), health_path ("/health"), max_connections (64),
  and an inner HttpHandlerConfig for CORS / body-size / timeout
  tuning.

- run_http_accept_loop: core accept loop shared by all run_http*
  entry-points. Binds a std TcpListener, wraps each accepted
  connection in BufReader/BufWriter + HttpTransport, then routes
  by path & method:
    POST /mcp   -> MCP JSON-RPC dispatch (handle_http_mcp_request)
    GET  /health -> {"status":"ok"} health-check
    OPTIONS /mcp -> CORS preflight via HttpRequestHandler
    *            -> 404

- handle_http_mcp_request: parses JSON-RPC from the HTTP body,
  renders request/response traffic to the console when enabled,
  tracks byte-level stats, dispatches through the existing
  Server::handle_request pipeline, and wraps the JSON-RPC response
  back into an HttpResponse (or 202 Accepted for notifications).

- render_http_startup_banner: HTTP-specific variant of the startup
  banner showing the listen address as "http://<addr>".

- Imports added: BufReader, BufWriter, TcpListener from std; plus
  HttpHandlerConfig, HttpMethod, HttpRequest, HttpRequestHandler,
  HttpResponse, HttpStatus, HttpTransport from fastmcp_transport::http.

crates/fastmcp/src/lib.rs re-exports HttpServerConfig from the
facade crate so downstream users can configure the HTTP server via
the top-level fastmcp import.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
